description: A type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's WebAssembly exception handling due to an inconsistency in how the null value is represented for the `kWasmNullExnRef` type. In `DefaultValueForType` within `src/wasm/constant-expression-interface.cc`, when initializing default values for `kRefNull`-kinded types, the condition checks for `kWasmExternRef`, `kWasmNullExternRef`, and `kWasmExnRef` to decide whether to use JS `null_value` vs. internal `wasm_null`. However, `kWasmNullExnRef` is absent from this condition, so table/struct/array elements of type `kWasmNullExnRef` are default-initialized with `wasm_null`. In contrast, `LoadNullValue` in `src/wasm/baseline/liftoff-compiler.cc` correctly includes `kWasmNullExnRef` in the condition that loads JS `null_value` for null comparisons. This mismatch means null checks on `kWasmNullExnRef` values compare against JS `null`, but the stored value is the internal `wasm_null` object, causing the check to fail. As a result, the internal `wasm_null` object is erroneously returned to the JavaScript layer, enabling attackers to obtain it and triggering type confusion. The bug was introduced by commit d09f312a14717fccc5ee0c4e8613bc83db4874d9 which added `kWasmNullExnRef` but did not update `DefaultValueForType` accordingly. Triggered by using WebAssembly tables or aggregates with element type `exnref`/`nullexnref` under the `--experimental-wasm-exnref` flag.

fixes.evidence_text: cf03d55db2a0b7c5ff62e08ff5ad52312f6da0b4 [wasm][exnref] Fix default value for null exnref
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/5444544 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
